title: rubus hirtus var. burnatii
intro: Rubus hirtus var. burnatii, also known as Burnat's raspberry, is a lesser-known variety of raspberry native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe. It is a deciduous shrub with edible fruits, which are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, and have been traditionally used for their medicinal and health benefits. The medicinal properties of Rubus hirtus var. burnatii include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as digestive issues and skin problems. Herbal preparations of Burnat's raspberry include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make jams, preserves, and other food products, while also providing health benefits when consumed as a supplement.
